title: Cyclopeltis kingii
taxonomy: Cyclopeltis kingii is a species of plant in the family Araliaceae, which belongs to the order Apiales. It is classified as a member of the genus Cyclopeltis. This species is native to tropical Asia and is characterized by its unique leaves and climbing habit. The classification of Cyclopeltis kingii places it within the kingdom Plantae, further subcategorized into the division Magnoliophyta. It is a part of the flowering plant family, known for its diverse species and adaptations.
uses: Cyclopeltis kingii is known for its medicinal properties in traditional medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia. The plant is used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. intro: Cyclopeltis kingii, also known as the Chinese water snake vine or cyclopeltis, is a plant species belonging to the family Lamiaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Cyclopeltis kingii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
